narrative: Your undergraduate GPA of 2.20 played the biggest role in the model's decision, as it stands well below the 3.24 average among students who went on to pass the bar exam on their first try, strongly pushing the prediction toward expecting you might struggle with the bar. Next, your LSAT score of 31 factored in significantly, again lower than the typical 37.27 seen in successful bar takers, further tipping the scales toward a higher risk of failure. On a more positive note, your Year 3 school decile ranking of 5 was a helpful counterbalance, slightly better than average for those who passed and nudging the prediction in favor of success. Overall, the model estimated a 60% chance you'll face challenges passing the bar exam right away, which is why your law school application was rejected—admissions prioritizes applicants predicted to pass with high confidence to ensure strong outcomes. Your lower-middle family income bracket, while not the top driver here, aligns with less common profiles among bar passers, where middle and upper-middle incomes dominate. Being a full-time student is a plus, matching the vast majority of those who succeed. Compared to typical accepted students, your academic metrics like GPA and LSAT are noticeably lower, placing you outside the stronger performers the model favors. Your Year 1 decile of 6 is right around average, so it didn't sway things much either way. This prediction isn't a judgment on your potential or background—it's just the model highlighting patterns from past students where these metrics strongly linked to bar results. To move forward, consider retaking the LSAT to boost your score closer to 37 or higher, and explore ways to strengthen your GPA through post-bac courses or additional undergrad achievements. Building a standout personal statement that showcases your resilience, perhaps drawing from your unique experiences, could also help in reapplying. Many students in similar spots improve these key areas and succeed—reach out to admissions advisors for targeted feedback on bolstering your profile. With focused effort, you can shift the odds in your favor for the next cycle.
